results.title: Dupely: The Trust Layer for Online Shopping - ProductDirs
results.content:
Dupely serves as a crucial trust layer for online shopping, aiming to empower consumers by combating common forms of manipulation. The platform is designed for shoppers who are wary of deceptive practices such as artificial price drops, fake reviews, and inflated markups from white-label sellers. Its core purpose is to provide transparency and confidence in every online purchase. Online shopping, while convenient, is often fraught with challenges that erode consumer trust. Shoppers frequently encounter misleading pricing strategies, inauthentic reviews that sway purchasing decisions, and influencers promoting products without genuine endorsement. Furthermore, the proliferation of resellers offering the same items at higher prices creates an environment where consumers struggle to find [...] ### Who is this for?

Dupely is designed for online shoppers who are concerned about deceptive practices and seek to make informed purchasing decisions. This includes consumers wary of artificial price drops, fake reviews, and inflated prices from resellers. It is particularly beneficial for individuals who frequently shop online and want to ensure they are getting genuine value and dealing with credible vendors. The tool aims to empower everyday shoppers, providing them with the confidence to navigate the complexities of e-commerce and avoid common pitfalls. [...] on iOS, Android, and Chrome extensions, with support for Brave and Edge browsers planned. The service is offered for free. The core team includes individuals like Jacob Galajda and Ryan Butz, who are actively involved in its development and community engagement. In essence, Dupely provides an essential layer of trust and transparency to online shopping, equipping consumers with the tools needed to navigate the complexities of e-commerce and make confident, value-driven purchases.

results.title: Dupely | aat.ee
results.content:
## About Dupely

### What is Dupely?

Dupely is a browser extension and mobile app that helps you stop overpaying on Amazon by finding identical products for less. Instead of chasing coupons or waiting for sales, Dupely scans product pages in real time to surface cheaper, verified duplicates — and flags whether a "deal" is actually a fake savings. It works on Chrome, Brave, iOS, and Android, acting as a trust layer for online shopping.

### Who it's for [...] Dupely | aat.ee

# Dupely

Dupely - Product Image

Most shopping tools chase coupons. Dupely tackles trust. Online shopping is full of manipulation: artificial price drops, fake reviews, paid influencers, and sketchy white label sellers reselling the same product at a markup. DupeScore finds identical products for less. Trust This Price flags fake savings using 90 days of price history. Seller badges show you who's actually credible. Now on iOS, Android, and Chrome. Dupely is the trust layer online shopping has been missing.

## About Dupely

### What is Dupely? [...] Most shopping tools are built around discounts and promo codes. Dupely is built around trust. It uses 90 days of price history to flag fake savings, identifies identical white-label products being resold at a markup, and scores each match so you know the dupe is real. It's not about finding a deal — it's about knowing you're not being tricked.

### Worth checking out if…

You shop on Amazon regularly and want to stop second-guessing whether you're paying too much. If you've ever seen a "70% off" badge and wondered if it's real, or found the same product listed under different brand names at wildly different prices, Dupely gives you the clarity you need — right inside your browser or phone.

results.title: Dupely: The trust layer for online shopping | Product Hunt | Ryan Butz | 12 comments
results.content:
Out of 1,053 products launched today, Dupely is #4 on Product Hunt!
Online shopping isn't broken by accident. It's been engineered against you.
If you've ever overpaid and felt it the second the package arrived, 𝐚𝐧 𝐮𝐩𝐯𝐨𝐭𝐞 𝐨𝐧 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐭 𝐇𝐮𝐧𝐭 𝐭𝐨𝐝𝐚𝐲 𝐠𝐨𝐞𝐬 𝐚 𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐰𝐚𝐲. 𝐏𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐞 𝐬𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐧𝐞𝐭𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤
I've spent decades selling on the world's biggest marketplaces, which means I've seen the manipulation from the inside.
→ Artificial price drops timed to fake a limited time sale.
→ Reviews written by people who got the product for free.
→ White-label sellers charging 40% more for the exact same item, from the exact same factory. [...] And every tool built to "help" shoppers has quietly accepted it as the rules of the game. Coupon codes inside a broken system. Cashback on overpriced products. Price alerts for prices that were never real.
We're in a trust recession.
We went a different direction.
Dupely is my answer on the shopping side.
Dupely finds the identical and near-identical product for less, verifies the seller, scores the match from 0 to 100, and flags fake sales using 90 days of real price history. It fires the second you land on an Amazon product page. No hunting. No extra tabs. No second-guessing whether the deal is real. [...] Dupely: Search for the same product but better prices | Product Hunt

Sarim Rayyan, graphic

Lowkey, comparison shopping shouldn't require detective-level skills. Congrats though

Guy Miotke, graphic

Top 10 out of 1,053 is no small feat — congrats to you and the Dupely crew. "Engineered against you" is exactly the right framing to build the counter-move around 👏

Julienne P., graphic

Absolutely incredible. Dupely is long overdue 🔥

results.content:
Last modified:  July 7, 2026  | Fred Magni

Ever felt that pang of buyer’s remorse, wondering if you overpaid for that sweet gadget or must-have accessory? Meet Dupely, the digital sidekick engineered to combat overpaying. It’s not just a tool; it’s your personal trust layer for online shopping, meticulously designed to unearth identical products at genuinely better prices across marketplaces, all without ever leaving your current page. Think of it as your shopping superpower, ensuring every click is a smart buy.

results.title: Ryan Butz - Building the trust layer behind online shopping | LinkedIn
results.content:
- 350,000 tracked events told us our core assumption about how people would use Dupely was wrong. Not the value. The behavior. Users were saving money. They just didn't default to shopping inside our app. Why would they? They've spent years learning how to navigate Amazon, Walmart, TikTok Shop. Thats muscle memory. It's the entire shopping experience. So we built the second option. Shop in the marketplaces you already use. Share to Dupely. Save money. Both paths exist now, but the share-in flow respects what users already know how to do. The lesson I'll carry into every product I build after this: if your thesis requires changing inherited user behavior, you're not building a product. You're funding a re-education campaign. The faster path is almost always meeting the user where they are [...] - Most of us have been overpaying online for years without even realizing it. Fake reviews, relabeled products, third-party sellers marking up the same factory item. It's become too normalized, and nobody was doing anything about it. So we built Dupely. Scan any listing and it instantly finds the real source, ranks the best alternatives, and shows you exactly what you're paying for. No 15+ open tabs, no digging, no 40 minutes of research. The average shopper saves $16 per purchase. We think that money should stay in your pocket. Available free on iOS, Android, and the Chrome Web Store ->  #SmartShopping #ConsumerTech #Dupely #SaveMoney #Ecommerce  
Ryan Butz reacted on this  
View Post [...] - We ran A LOT of studies and did extensive market research. As we approach our first 1k installs, I check the logs to see our users actually using Dupely to help them save at checkout. The coupons, promo codes and price drop apps all try to get you to buy the overpriced product. Dupely finds identical or mostly similar products at a much lower price. On average, we currently save users $16 at checkout. $16  1,000 users = $16k saved within a single month. Check it out, it's totally free (you don't even need to give us your email).
